full_response:
We start with an empty line.

Notation  
‣ L_t = number of people in line at the **end** of minute t (after Barr has had a chance to serve one person in that minute).  
‣ Arrivals in a minute:  

 with probability p → 2 friends arrive,  
 with probability 1-p → 0 friends arrive.

Minute-by-minute evolution
--------------------------------------------------------------------
Minute 1  
• If 0 friends arrive (probability 1-p):  
 L₁ = 0 (nobody to serve).

• If 2 friends arrive (probability p):  
 Line becomes 2; Barr serves 1 of them;  
 L₁ = 1.

Minute 2 – branch on the value of L₁
--------------------------------------------------------------------
1. Branch L₁ = 0 (this branch occurs with probability 1-p)

 a. 0 friends arrive (prob. 1-p): L₂ = 0 ––> contributes (1-p)(1-p).  
 b. 2 friends arrive (prob. p):  L₂ = 1 ––> not empty.

 Probability that line is empty at t = 2 from this branch = (1-p)(1-p).

2. Branch L₁ = 1 (this branch occurs with probability p)

 a. 0 friends arrive (prob. 1-p): Barr serves the 1 waiting person → L₂ = 0 ––> contributes p(1-p).  
 b. 2 friends arrive (prob. p):  Line briefly 3, Barr serves 1 → L₂ = 2 ––> not empty.

 Probability that line is empty at t = 2 from this branch = p(1-p).

Combine the two branches
--------------------------------------------------------------------
P[L₂ = 0] = (1-p)(1-p) + p(1-p)  
     = (1-p)[(1-p) + p]  
     = (1-p)·1  
     = 1-p.

Hence the probability that the line is empty after two minutes is 1-p.

\boxed{1-p}

Confidence: 0.82
